MAIL4 (BRIDGE)



Hi [NAME], did you enjoy the three-part email series?



In these past three emails, you discovered and learned a lot about the benefits of nature and walking. 



The effects those have on clients and professionals.



The differences between indoors vs outdoors.



And about ways to use nature during the process of helping others. To create even more impact.



I hope nature's magical and beneficial ways inspired you.



And make you wonder about the possibilities out there for yourself. To impact your clients at an even deeper level.



Like Corné's ticket to freedom through that wooden fence, which was the fitting metaphor at the right time, these magical experiences happen all the time.



Peter and I could write an inspiring book about that. "The Mysterious Ways of Mother Nature Helping Others!"



That's what we love most about this impactful way of helping others.



Through the years, I helped lots of people in nature. Really feeling blessed! Finding my own path.



Specialising in coaching for men. Helping them with personal problems, emotions, manhood, career, relationships and purpose in life.



Together with my wife, we started helping couples in nature with their relationship problems.



During that process of finding my own path, I realised that every method we learned could be integrated into nature.



For example, a therapy we learned called 'EFT'. 'Emotionally Focused Therapy' from Dr Sue Johnson.



And also with a technique called 'Voice Dialogue' from psychologists Hal & Sidra Stone.



My wife and I found ways to apply those methods in nature. Combined with the benefits of nature and walking, and the way we use nature.



What makes me think again about that journey with Peter. 



Three years ago, I made a real stunning discovery.



I read a lot about mental health issues being #1 in many countries, like in the UK.



Enthusiastic, I thought about all those powerful natural benefits and possibilities, like stress releasing effects and positive effects on well-being. Being the right ingredients to solve those kinds of problems.



So I Googled to find some colleagues around the world that also use nature.



Strangely, I couldn't find any …



"Huh?! How is that possible?" I thought. Here in the Netherlands, our method is common ground.



So I started to do some research internationally.



But I couldn't find people, except for some lone wolves, who worked with nature. According to the method, Peter developed. Nowhere!



Yes, I found things like Shamanism, of course.



But not a method based on coaching and therapy. And no programs for professionals to learn.



So immediately, I called Peter.



Thinking about that moment eight years ago that I left ATMA with my certificate in my pocket.



Intuitively, back then, I felt that my time with ATMA wasn't finished yet.



It felt like family. The moment the first time I walked through their front door.



So I said: "Peter! Remember that I told you eight years ago that I would come back to be part of that ATMA family?".



"Yes, I remember Coen."



"Well … this is the moment, my friend!"



"You and I really need to go for a walk in nature and talk about things."



So we did.



During that walk, I told Peter about what I found out. And how I thought about that.



And convinced him that we really should bring this method to the world. Raising the standard of helping professions. Raise the impact on clients.



During our walk, Peter and I used nature's synchronicities (and my enthusiasm) to shake hands on bringing this method to the world.







This method we call 'Outdoor Life Coaching'.



And to be honest, I still don't understand that this kind of method is unique and nowhere taught in the world.



Feeling even more blessed, Peter crossed my path.



Imagine what we all could do about those #1 mental health issues, for instance.



Anyway …



Peter and I took a couple of walks to let nature show us the way. Using our method.



Nature showed: Start in Europe (with coloured pathway flags on trees). Make it really easy for professionals to learn the basics. And to open up the possibility to integrate our method into other professions.



So that's what we did.



We cut to the core so we could teach our method in just one day. And after that with a two-day advanced training.



Real easy to learn. Yet very powerful.



And we started two years ago with just one Facebook ad.



And now the funny part …



Remember Andrea?



(The top coach that felt her mind-blowing experience in nature was HUGE and full of potential.)



She couldn't find any outdoor training programs. Other than shamanistic approaches. Or going back to basic nature with a tent, leaving everyday life behind.



That wasn't what she was looking for.



She wanted a more western and scientific approach.



One that could support her expertise and profession.



So her journey ended right there behind her laptop.



While in the background, that feeling of yearning for something more profound was still there.



For one year …



… But then after 1 year … 



** PLOP ***



A Facebook message appeared on her screen.



SURPRISED!



She tracked us down. Found us in the Netherlands.



And there it was …



… this unique and powerful outdoor method.



Easy to learn.



"Very different to any other thing I ever saw.", she said.



Immediately, seeing possibilities to be unique.



Being a pioneer.



Coaching outdoors.



Combining things she loves most.



Making impact.



And being in nature.



But it takes guts.



Integrating this method isn't for the ones who want to play by the rules and culture.



You need to have the courage to go outside (the box).



Take clients outside.



Dare to be a pioneer on this.



See for yourself.



Experience those benefits out there.



Maybe within your organisation, you need to set the standard.



Regardless of what colleagues think or say.



On the other hand, think again about indoors versus outdoors.



Think about those health benefits versus sitting in a non-natural environment.



Think about that.



For me, this way of working with nature doesn't feel like the typical 'new method' or 'upgrade' to your profession.



Not just another method or tool to put in your backpack.



NO … it feels like A WHOLE NEW BACKPACK. Where you can put all your expertise, tools and methods in.



Possibilities are endless, just like the infinite diversity of nature itself.



Imagine what you can do out there. Being creative.



And it's still going on …



Last year, because of COVID, Peter created a new therapeutic method to use walking outside to process trauma.



Again, unique how a special kind of walking supports that process.



Possibilities are endless.



That's what I discovered for over ten years now, and Peter for more than twenty.



It's our journey to take other helping professionals there and unleash all those hidden powers within them.



For the benefits of their clients and themselves. Professionally and personally.



We want to raise the standard for helping professions.



And at this moment, there are plenty of opportunities in many countries because our method is new.



Unique.



So we want to give you also the opportunity to learn our method.



But only if you feel like working outdoors could really impact the results of your clients and yourself.



If you want to know more about Outdoor Life Coaching, then raise your hand.



Let us know you are one of those potential pioneers.



If so, click on this link [link + thank you! More info to come-page].



If this is not for you, then please don't click the link.



But if you do, I will send you two more emails.



With in-depth information about the method itself, what's in the online box and an offer (€197) to participate in our online Introductory Training Course.



Either how I want to thank you for being on this journey with me.



And for your time to read through these emails.



I really appreciate it!









Kind regards,



[PHOTO]



Coen van Broekhoven

Life coach, relationship coach & trainer





MAIL5 (PROMO1)



Hi [NAME], 



Great, you're one of those potential pioneers that raised their hands.



The last few days, we talked about the excellent benefits of nature and walking. And about our method to use outdoors in the coaching and therapeutic process.



Today and tomorrow are all about discovering what our online introductory course to 'Outdoor Life Coaching' will bring you.



But before we dive in, I first want to share our journey with you about how this introductory course came about.



10 years ago, I followed the two-year program of Peter myself.

Because of nature constantly mirroring and providing Peter with new insights, he simplified his training program through the years. Bringing it back to its true nature.





When we high-fived on bringing our method to the world, we talked and thought about how we wanted to do that.



Our goal was to make our teachings as easy to learn and apply as possible to other professionals.



That's why we challenged ourselves to cut to the core of Outdoor Coaching. To higher impact even more.



From that thinking process, our concept was born.



A 1-day live training course in which you learn all the powerful basics, which I will go into later on in this email, needed to immediately coach yourself. 



An in-depth 2-day advanced live training course. For those who want to go one step further. You learn how to apply the basics, especially on career, relationships and stress release. And learn new techniques as well.



And we created a third expert training and very advanced techniques, like using 'voice dialogue' (working with sub-personalities, founded by Sidra & Hall Stone) in nature. Which we'll offer in the near future.



But only learning the basics is enough to immediately start coaching in nature or implement it in your own profession right away. There's no need to advance. Only if you want to.



We took a shot and launched one Facebook ad.



We got some excited counsellors, coaches and trainers from different countries, like Wales, England, Ireland, Germany, Hungary, Switzerland.



It led to an introductory training course in Wales and England. And a three-day introductory and advanced training course in the Netherlands.



Exciting, and a lot of fun :)



The Netherlands is flat, so working with hills, mountains and rivers gave another dimension to the experience. And magic started happening immediately …



Like Oliver, a senior counsellor and supervisor from Wales, he attended our 1-day training course in Wales. While upfront being open about being very sceptical.



During a demo, I coached him while reflecting upon a beautiful landscape in the Brecon Beacons, a well-known area in Wales. The landscape had a green, open field full of grass going uphill. Surrounded by higher oak trees. In the back, some bushes bordered the area with the forest behind it. And in the distance, a giant hill full of den groves rose above it.



Long story short … it really struck him when he discovered that hill symbolizing his higher goal in life.



After the course, he immediately started taking clients out and integrated the basics into his counselling. 6 months after that, he started his own concept: Outdoor Counselling.



Peter and I were on a journey. That was all about supporting international coaches, counsellors and therapists. To make an impact, doing what they love most on an even higher level in the area they love (nature). The most beautiful and inspiring office in the world :)



And then COVID came …



That's when things got rough because of border restrictions. 



Our live training courses had to be postponed.



That's when we decided to make our training courses available online with precisely the same content as our live training courses. To keep supporting and inspiring other coaches, counsellors and therapists.



So that brings us back to our online introductory course.

In which you will learn to make an impact on your clients using nature.



The content consists of 43 how-to and coaching demo videos. In which you'll learn all the basics you need so you can start immediately with coaching in nature.



It's essential to go outside to practise and experience.

That's why you get 19 different exercises. The first two you get already in chapter two. So you immediately start practising, sharing and integrating the basics. We advise you to practise with clients. Or with family, friends or colleagues. (or both.)



Next to that, you get a beginner's guide about Outdoor Life Coaching in general. A basic questions sheet. And a reader specifically about the training course. Full of different subjects and examples of coaching sessions. So you get even more insights on Outdoor Life Coaching.



We can imagine that practising and later on taking clients with you will lead to questions and a need for feedback. That's why we added a 2-monthly free online Q&A of 1 hour. Where you get support from us personally. These Q&A's are recorded and added in audio to the training course.



You get lifetime access to all of this to keep learning and growing. And you also get a certificate that gives clients proof that you are well educated.



So what are those basics that you will learn during the training course?







During the training course, you will learn more about the benefits and what happens when nature participates in the process. So you get a deep understanding of what's happening and how nature impacts yourself and your clients.



You learn the 3 basic questions that let you integrate nature and bring inspiration into your coaching, counselling or therapeutic process anytime and anywhere. Using these questions will also help you to break the possible pattern of hiding behind mental stories. And make your process very visual for your clients, which will help them memorize the insights and breakthroughs they get in the future, long after you helped them. 

  

You will learn to use different walks to provide you with lots of various opportunities during coaching sessions.



During a session, you will encounter different natural elements on your path that are usable. Like splits, junctions, puddles and obstacles (fences, fallen trees, etc.) 







You will learn to work with them in compelling ways. So you can make a deep impact on clients that will lead to profound, long-lasting results.



Next to those, you will also learn to use landscapes, trees and other small natural elements.



Finally, we will share ideas with you on how to use what you have learned. To inspire you to integrate our method or use it standalone.



And if you want to learn even more, going through the introductory course will open up the possibility to participate in the advanced course as well.



After following all the videos, read the documentary, do the exercises, you will be a certified outdoor life coach and start making nature your everyday practice. Being surrounded by all those (health) benefits yourself while making a profound impact on clients.



I hope you got a clear vision of what adventure our introductory course will take you on.



Tomorrow I will send you my last email.



And will let you in on a little secret I discovered with my own clients.



See you tomorrow!







Kind regards,



[PHOTO]



Coen van Broekhoven

Life coach, relationship coach & trainer







PS. If you want to participate right now you can. The introductory course is €197. Here's the link: https://outdoorlifecoaching.com/cart/





MAIL6 (PROMO2)



Today is our last day together [NAME].



We went on an adventure to give you an inside view of what's possible for you in nature.



Impact clients while working in the most inspiring and beneficial office in the world. Where possibilities are endless. And lots of problems can be solved. Mentally, emotionally and physically.



You learned so much the past few days. And the exciting thing is that our method is only well-known in the Netherlands.



This makes possibilities even greater for yourself. Being unique in your marketplace. Because using nature will make you more specific and attract clients who will also love being in nature.



That little secret I would reveal is that almost all my clients say they really need to go out more into nature after just 1 walk. That they miss it. And liked it so much in the past.



They feel reborn after just 1 session, just by being there.



Think about that.



Next week you could already be coaching in nature. Experiencing the impact of nature and our method. Being inspired all the time. Feeling healthy. And amazed about the magic happening there.



If you want to be a pioneer, learn, grow and make an impact, you can join us today for €197. With lifetime access. And 2-monthly online support.



If so: click this link and register.https://outdoorlifecoaching.com/cart/



Thanks again for our journey together.



It was my pleasure!









Kind regards,



[PHOTO]



Coen van Broekhoven

Life coach, relationship coach & trainer







PS. If you want to participate right now, here's the link.

https://outdoorlifecoaching.com/cart/









MAIL1



Great! That you're here [NAME].

I'm happy that we continue our adventurous journey together :)



The next three days are all about making an impact on clients using nature.



You already discovered the mental, emotional and physical impact and health benefits of nature, and walking, on clients and yourself.



Keep in mind that this benefit-frame is always there when entering 'the Green Arena'.



But what about the helping process itself?



That's where we, helping professionals, get really excited. We want to grow. We want to learn. We want to help. Better the world. We want to improve ourselves, so we can contribute even more.



These three days you will discover:

* How nature can be integrated into this process.

* How integration opens up a treasure chest full of creative possibilities,

* The impact this has on clients' results.



Today's email is about how Peter Kryger discovered a way to integrate nature.



Tomorrow we will go into the powerful elements that nature provides for the helping process.



And the last email will shine a light on synchronicities in nature that impact the results. 



Ten years ago, it all started …



Back then, he was one of my Dutch teachers. Thankful to this day that I met him and learned from his wisdom.



So who is this guy?



(Or better yet … who is this very VERY experienced, well-educated and wise 60+-years old therapist, coach & teacher.)



Peter lives in THE most beautiful natural area of the Netherlands, 'de Veluwe'. Well-known for its beautiful diverse nature. Full of wildlife. And endless kilometres of purple hay in August.



But let's go back in time …



.



.



Because his journey started 40 years ago when he ran up a hill near Canterbury. After just finishing his study Hypnotherapy in England.



Standing on top of that hill, he got a deep insight.



Words came and he shouted out loud, from his toes:



"I WILL TEACH OTHERS TO BECOME HYPNOTHERAPISTS IN THE NETHERLANDS!"



And that's what he did … 



Back in the Netherlands, he started teaching.



Years went by and in 1989 he founded his institute for therapy: ATMA.



Through the years, ATMA grew, and grew.



Now, ATMA has over 31 years of experience in practical training and education in the field of personal development and helping professions.



Over the years, Peter and his teachers (23) taught more than 10.000 students different kinds of therapy and coaching programs. All NOBCO-certified (Dutch ICF).



1 to 3 year-long programs. Like: Hypnotherapy. Life coaching. Child coaching. Relationship coaching. Vitality coaching. Burnout specialism. Body-oriënted coaching and therapy. Trauma therapy. Holistic therapy.



And lots of shorter training courses.



Most programs Peter created himself.



In the early days, Peter lived at his apartment in the centre of a city called Amersfoort (1-hour drive from Amsterdam). Surrounded by beautiful nature.



Because Peter likes nature and lived in his apartment in the city, he always went there during and after work. Enjoying the beautiful forest.



Laying down in the grass. Or sitting on his folding chair.



Listening to the beautiful sounds of the birds. Watching the different colours in nature. Feeling the sun warming his skin.



Just enjoying the silence of being there. Or walking through the woods.



It felt like his own backyard.



He loved it. Also on the weekends.



As years went by he became more and more experienced as a therapist, coach and teacher.



And then, twenty years ago …



… something natural happened.



Intuitively, inspired by nature, he started taking clients there. Loved to be there anyway.



And after a while, he also took his students there.



Enjoying nature while doing therapy and coaching.



Clients and students loved it.







That was a lot of fun. But it was only after time the real magic emerged.



Because what happened was …



Peter started to notice that nature itself influenced the process. And not only that.



He started to experience nature influencing himself as well.



His creativity was stimulated. He felt inspired.



All kinds of possibilities presented themselves naturally.



It felt healthy and enjoyable.



Synchronicities started to happen during coaching and therapy.



Processes went deeper.



Clients easily opened up. They liked it. They felt safe.



Stress released naturally.



Insights and results came real fast. 



Differences between working indoors and outdoors became visible for him.



While doing this, Peter began to see endless possibilities. Just like the endless diversity of nature itself.



He started experimenting. 



Discovered lots of different natural elements to work with.



For the purpose of different areas of life. And different kinds of problems.



Also using different techniques from different helping professions.



Step by step, working with nature was born.



Eventually, Peter created an amazing two-year program. Teaching other professionals the possibilities to work with and in nature.



These days he only teaches outdoor techniques. Because that’s what he loves most and finds most effective.



I went through that program ten years ago myself. After already working for two years as an energetic therapist.



And work with clients in nature ever since. Using all my expertise. Different coaching methods. And therapeutic skills.



(The last two years also teach others, within ATMA, how to work in and with nature. The additional two-year program kept evolving. And is now a one to five-day program with powerful simplicity.)



While Peter went on his own outdoor adventure through the past decades, all the scientific research on the positive effects of nature and walking on humans also took place.



All that knowledge combined, gave us much more understanding of the magical results Peter discovered from the very beginning.



Well … actually it gave me much more understanding. Peter had lots already. Hahaaa...



But he also learned new things. For instance, from the Shinrin-yoku experiments.



This story continues and gets even better. 



Because ten years ago, Peter and I went on a journey together, without him knowing it. (Funny …)



More about that in the last email.



Tomorrow we will go in-depth on what happens when nature is invited to helping professions. And into those powerful natural elements that you can use.



See you tomorrow!



Kind regards,







Coen van Broekhoven

Life coach, relationship coach & trainer





MAIL2



Hi again.



A green welcome in today's email.



Yesterday was about Peter finding a very powerful way to use nature and all its benefits as a helping professional. Even developed a method and training program about that.



Today we will dive into what actually happens when you start using nature and what's possible out there.



To do that, we first have to take a step back and look from a higher perspective to what's actually going during the process, when we start using nature.



And then look at the perspective of the client.



It's really amazing what happens!



Because nature itself becomes part of the process.



And therefore a CCE triangle arises.



CCE as in Coach Client Environment.







Working indoors there's only interaction between the professional and the

client.



Already very enriching.



Because, through questions and interventions, the professional penetrates to deeper levels of the inner world of the client.



Outdoors, however, involves the environment.



As a result, both the client and the professional coach are highly inspired.



Everything in the environment can be used as metaphors. That symbolically illustrates, broadens and deepens the topics of the process.



So what happens is that triangle that nature starts to be an inspiring second coach.



After all those years I am still amazed every time about the events that happen during a session.



And then there's the clients' perspective.



Nature helps to reflect the client's inner process.



For example, while a client tells about a struggle in his/her life, you both are walking uphill. The client feels the struggle walking uphill. The path represents that.



Or there are clouds that darken the environment and symbolise that struggle and even the emotions that go with that.



Pure Magic!



By walking regularly during a session, you bring variation to the conversation because you always end up in a different environment.



Walking is also important to get the body moving, which causes the thoughts, feelings and energy to move. And it stimulates the brain as well.



And meanwhile, all those others benefits, as discovered through scientific research, are also affecting the whole process. And deepen the process that's going on.



Which makes the client open up more easily.



Using the environment as a mirror makes it easier for clients to get clarity on their inner process. The symbolism of nature makes it clear and visual. Which makes results go fast and deep in a natural way.



During that natural process together, all kinds of elements can be used.



For example, you can use a tree as a metaphor for someone's personality. Or a landscape for the structure of an organisation.



Paths can be seen as metaphors for someone’s career or life. With obstacles, puddles and junctions along the way.



Animals can be used as a source of

inspiration for strength, liveliness or playfulness.



You can do creative work forms and rituals with objects from the environment that initiate psychological processes.



Everything is possible out there. Everything!



Just like that time with one of my clients, Corne.



It was the fourth (of twelve) coaching session.



(All my clients go through a transformational twelve-session coaching program.)



We were walking and talking about how things went the last few weeks.



Out of the sudden a wooden fence appeared on the path.







Exactly at the moment that Corne talked about his tendency to avoid obstacles in his life.



He stood still, felt that tendency and kind of froze.



While deep down a longing for more adventure in his life lured.



Behind us, a beautiful coloured landscape. Full of purple hay and trees. With a wide view. 



To the right, an open swamp area. With the blue sky reflected in the puddles.



In front, behind the fence, a narrow path - bounded by trees - led into the distance.



He couldn't see where that path would lead him. But it felt adventurous. He could feel that longing inside getting stronger.



Left and right of the wooden fence was barbed wire. Separating the landscape and the path.



While staring at that fence, he discovered that the fence symbolized his ticket to freedom.



Exactly the freedom he was looking for in his life. For a long time …



After years of painfully hindering himself. He felt that pain immediately through his whole body.



The pain from fifty years not having the guts to explore life on his own.



50 years of doing things (he thought) others expect from him.



'Doing the right thing', and therefore feeling oppressed all the time. Like a bird in a cage, sitting quietly and depressed in a corner, singing whenever someone looked at the cage.



Now before that fence he realized that he kept himself sitting inside that cage all that time, while the door was open. *AUCH*



That moment, nature mirrored exactly what he always did in life when occurring obstacles. 

Looking at those obstacles. Standing still. Unconsciously that is …



Standing there and thinking about what he had to do. Dreaming about what he wanted. Meanwhile standing still and NOT moving forward.



That moment represented the story of his life.



His body tended to walk back, away from that fence. Just like he always did. Till the next obstacle popped up.



Year after year he felt limp, disproved, powerless … Not a man at all …



But not anymore. Now, adventure was calling … SHOUTING! Pulling on him.



At that very moment, he decided to walk through that fence. No more turning back.



He took 2 steps forward. Reached with his hands for the fence. And opened it …



* SIGH *



He walked through.



I could see him standing up right, with every step he took.



"COEN! THIS IS ME!", he shouted with a big relief. "This is me man!"



Fresh air filling his lungs. Waving his hands and arms freely. Feeling that deep sense of freedom.



For the first time he felt lots of space.



But … 



After ten metres, he heard those old voices in his head again, saying:



"Don't be different than others.". What his parents told him early on.



"You need to work till sixty, then retire." . That's what his father also did, with the same job, at the same factory.



A feeling of worry rushed through his body.



Those voices dominated great parts of his life for many years, unconsciously.



But now he had this new experience. Breaking through that fence. Feelings of freedom.



Now he heard those voices consciously this time.



"Free … YES!!!" He yelled.



And walked further down that path.



While walking he got insights.



About camping. About buying a motorhome. About working less and in a few years stopping entirely.



About going out to see the world. To the Dutch sea. In cold winters going to the warm-hearted people in Spain and Portugal.



He could feel the aliveness already rushing through his vains.



Two weeks later we met again for our next session.



I just parked my car at the forest parking lot.



Still sitting in my car, the door flew open.



"Coen, I booked a motorhome yesterday! I really feel alive man, and ignited, since our last session."



For me, that wooden fence unleashing those patterns and insights and the possibility to break through is true NATURE'S MAGIC!



Can you imagine how this would be for you and your clients, taking them outside?



That’s it for today.



Tomorrow you will discover about synchronicities and how reading about mental health issues accelerated the adventure with Peter.



See you tomorrow!



Kind regards,







Coen van Broekhoven

Life coach, relationship coach & trainer





MAIL3



Getting inspired by nature already?



I'm happy you're still here, so I can share some more thoughts on the impact of nature with you, in today's last email.



Yesterday you read about Corne's ticket to freedom. Through that wooden fence.



This kind of experience happens all the time to our clients. That's what Peter and I like most about this way of helping others. Feeling very inspired and thankful to be part of that.



Through the years we discovered that being in nature, opened up our own intuition and creativity.



When being in nature, synchronicities fly all over the place. Like that fence that appeared at the exact right moment.



Or rabbits shooting out of the woods, when clients talk about anxiety.



The sun starts to shine when a client finds inner strength. Or horses appear out of nowhere.



Raindrops fall out of the sky when tears come up. And stop, at the exact moment when clients stop crying.



The wind starts to blow when clients start to feel energized.



Junctions appear when clients tell about decisions they need to make in their lives, or when they want to take a different path. 



To name a few.



One time the sun broke through the trees, right on the forehead of a client. At the exact moment, she found self-confidence again. I was scratching my forehead. Wondering how the hell that sun could get through at all. Since we stood so deep in the forest, surrounded by trees.



When those synchronicities start taking place, that's when the real magic of nature hits you. Well, it did with me.



Through the years I started coaching lots of people in nature. Found my own path. Specialized in coaching men. Helping them with problems in life, manhood, career, relationships and purpose in life.



After a while, together with my wife, we also started coaching couples in nature helping them with relationship problems.



In that process, I started to understand that every method I learned was possible to integrate into nature.



For example, a new therapy I learned called EFT, emotionally focused therapy from Dr Sue Johnson. My wife and I found ways to apply that in nature. Next to other relationship techniques.



Also use a lot of Voice Dialogue (Hal & Sidra Stone) in nature. Using trees to set the stage for sub-personalities.



Inspiring to create new ways. But the basics from what I learned ten years ago from Peter, always will set the frame.



Together with the benefits of nature and walking. Like research has shown through the years. And I experience everytime myself, when I’m there.



For me, this way of working with nature doesn't feel like the typical 'new method' or 'upgrade' to your profession. Not just another method or tool to put in your backpack.



No … it feels like A WHOLE NEW BACKPACK. Where you can put all your expertise, tools and methods in. 



Other helping professionals, I taught, feel so too. They are amazed about the simplicity of it. And what they experience when practicing. Being client and professional.







Possibilities are endless, just like the endless diversity of nature itself.



Use your creativity, and that will happen naturally in nature, and everything is possible. Imagine what you can do out there with what you already know and use yourself.



And it's still going on …



Last year, because of COVID, Peter created a new therapeutic method to use walking outside for the processing of trauma. Again, amazing how a special kind of walking supports that process.



Possibilities are endless. That's what I discovered for over ten years now, and Peter for more than twenty.



What makes me think again about that journey with Peter. 



Three years ago I made a real stunning discovery.



I was reading a lot about mental health issues being #1 in lots of countries these days. Like in the UK.



Enthusiastic, I thought about all those powerful natural benefits and possibilities, helping people with that.



So I Googled to find some colleagues around the world that also used nature.



I couldn't find any. "Strange!"



"Huh?! How is that possible?", I thought. Here in the Netherlands, this method is common ground.



So I started to do some research internationally.



But nowhere, outside some lone wolves, I couldn't find people who worked with nature, according to the method Peter developed.



Yes, I found things like Shamanism. But not a method based on coaching and therapy. And no programs for professionals to learn.



So immediately, I called Peter. Thinking about that moment eight years ago that I left ATMA with my outdoor life coach certificate in my pocket.



Intuitively, I felt then that my time with ATMA wasn't finished yet. It felt like family, the moment for the first time I walked through their front door.



So I said: "Peter! Remember that I told you eight years ago, that I would come back to be part of the ATMA family?".



"Yes, I remember Coen."



"Well … this is the moment! You and I really need to go for a walk in nature and talk about outdoor life coaching".



So we did.



During that walk, Peter and I used nature's synchronicities, and my enthusiasm, to shake hands on bringing this way of using nature, called Outdoor Life Coaching to the world.



And to be honest, till this moment I cannot understand that this method is unique and nowhere taught in the world.



Imagine what we all could do about those #1 mental health issues, for instance.



Peter and I took a couple of walks to let nature show us the way.



Nature said: Start in Europe and make it really easy for professionals to learn the basics and also make it possible to integrate into other professions.



So that's what we did and started two years ago. With one Facebook ad.



What's funny …



Remember Andrea?



The German top coach felt her mind blowing experience in nature was HUGE and full of potential.



She couldn't find anything, other than shamanistic approaches or going back to basic, in nature with a tent, leaving everyday life behind.



That wasn't what she was looking for.



She wanted a more western and scientific approach. One that could support her expertise and profession as a coach.



So her journey ended there.



While in the background that feeling of yearning for something deeper was still there. For one year …



… But then after 1 year … 



** PLOP ***



A Facebook message appeared on her screen.



SURPRISED!



She tracked us down, found us in the Netherlands.



And there it was …



… this unique and powerful outdoor method. Easy to learn.



"Very different to any other thing I ever saw.", she said.



Immediately, seeing possibilities to be unique. Being a pioneer. Coaching outdoors. Combining things she loves most. Making an impact. And being in nature.



Like Andrea, you know now after these three emails, how nature can be integrated, how integration opens up a treasure chest full of creative possibilities and the impact this has on clients' results.



It's Peters and my journey to take you there and unleash all those hidden powers within you. For the benefits of your clients and yourself. Professionally. Personally.



We want to raise the standard for helping professions. And at this moment, there are plenty of opportunities in lots of countries, because our method is new and unique.



But it takes guts.



Integrating this method isn't for the ones who want to play it by the rules and culture.



You need to have the courage to go outside (the box).



Take clients outside. Dare to be a pioneer on this. See for yourself. Experience those benefits out there.



Maybe within your organisation, you need to set the standard. Regardless of what colleagues think or say.



On the other hand, think again about indoors versus outdoors. Think about those health benefits versus sitting and being in a non-natural environment. Think about that.



So if you want to know more about our method ... raise your hand. Let us know you are one of those (potential) pioneers.



Click on this link, and I will send you three more emails with in-depth information about the method (www.outdoorlifecoaching.com) itself. With the chance to participate.



Either how, I hope you'll take clients out and start to explore. Experience the difference.



Let me know if you do.



Thanks for being on this journey with me.



For your time to read through these emails.



Really appreciate it!



Kind regards,







Coen van Broekhoven

Life coach, relationship coach & trainer











LANDING PAGE



What if helping professionals could make much more impact on their clients while being in the environment they love most?



Well …



Andrea had this mind blowing experience. While walking and talking in nature.



An experience that felt so deep and yet so easy.



Something she experienced never before.



She didn't know exactly what it was or what happened.



But she felt immediately that this could be something powerful.

Using all of her abilities, techniques and experience in nature somehow.



In fact … it is!



When helping professionals start to take their clients out into nature, this will have an enormous impact. Not only on the process and their clients' results. Also on themselves.



Andreas' experience had three important elements in it that produced this result.

Without her knowing it.



Nature.



Walking.



And the Effect both had.



"Easy and deep, in a natural way.", she said.

While talking about an unconventional subject like 'sex'.



Let's dive into these three things.

To understand more deeply why she had this experience.



Beginning with nature itself.



Lots of things are going while being there. 

But for now, let's focus on scientific research. Especially on Dr.Qing Li.



A very "natural guy" you could say.



Being a Japanese professor and medical doctor at Tokyo’s Nippon Medical School.

Visiting fellow at the Stanford University School of Medicine.

Founding member and chairman of the Japanese Society for Forest Medicine.

Leading member of the Task Force of Forests and Human Health.

And vice president and secretary general of the International Society of Nature and Forest Medicine.



Very natural.



For decades, he and his colleagues did lots of experiments in natural environments and non-natural environments.

Experiments like walking and sitting in nature.



Also doing the same activities in non-natural environments.

To measure the differences. 



What he discovered was ENORMOUS.



Nature provides us many benefits on different kinds of health.

Benefits like: Reduced stress. Lower blood pressure. Improved mood. Increased focus and energy.



Where non-natural environments did not. They produced stress instead.



Every experiment, they measured that our body physiologically relaxes when being in nature.

And did not measure these results when doing the same activities in non-natural environments.



But not only that.



The subjects also sensed a stronger feeling of well-being. Felt more relaxed. More energetic. Alive. Fresh.

And they experienced a better emotional balance and decrease of fear.



Amazing, right!



Even when subjects didn't walk in nature but only sat down and watched nature, they measured positive results.



And it gets better and better …



The positive effects of nature on the subjects were still there after one month. ONE MONTH!



He also discovered that trees breathe out substances called 'Phytoncide'. Which again have positive effects on human stress levels. 

So he started doing research on making 'forest medicine'. Which resulted in first positive effects on diseases like some types of cancer.



And this is just a tiny fraction of what he discovered.



His decades of scientific research on the effects of nature on humans led to a nationwide integrated therapy in Japan. 

For the well-being of their citizens. This therapy is called "Shinrin-yoku" ("Forest Bathing").

Maybe you heard of it already. It's finding its way to other countries.



And that's just ONE example of many kinds of studies around the world.



Just like the study of 20.000 people, led by Mathew White of the European Centre for Environment & Human Health at the University of Exeter.



His team found that people who spent two hours a week in green spaces were substantially more likely to report good health and psychological well-being than those who didn’t.

Either they spent their two hours all at once. Or spaced over several visits.



The effects were robust. Cutting across different occupations. Ethnic groups. People from rich and poor areas.

And people with chronic illnesses and disabilities.



So research shows the enormous impact nature has on us humans and our health.

Walking. Spending time. Sitting. Doesn't matter.



But what about the walking itself?



Exercise experts, doctors, psychologists etc. advise people  a lot to go for a walk.

Especially those who have too little exercise in their lives, experience stress or have mental health issues.



There are very good reasons why.

As shown by lots of studies on the effects of walking on humans.



"Scientific research shows that placing one foot in front of the other leads to some seriously impressive mental and physical benefits."

(article NBC News)

 

A couple of different studies showed that you may be missing out on an extremely effective physical activity, not to mention a mental boost, by not walking entirely.



Physical benefits: significant improvements in blood pressure, slowing of resting heart rate, reduction of body fat and body weight, reduced cholesterol, improved depression scores with better quality of life and increased measures of endurance.



Mental benefits: increased creative output by an average of sixty percent.

Researchers labelled this type of creativity “divergent thinking,” which they define as a thought process used to generate creative ideas by exploring many possible solutions.



According to a study about the mental aspects, “walking opens up the free flow of ideas. And it is a simple and robust solution to the goals of increasing creativity and increasing physical activity.”



Psychologists found that a ten minute walk may be just as good as a forty five minute workout when it comes to relieving the symptoms of anxiety.



But it’s not only your creativity that will benefit from the mental lift. 



The act of walking is also a proven mood booster.



One study found that just twelve minutes of walking resulted in an increase in joviality, vigor, attentiveness and self-confidence versus the same time spent sitting.



(Remember … our sitting culture!)



Walking in nature, specifically, was found to reduce ruminating over negative experiences, which increases activity in the brain associated with negative emotions and raises risk of depression.



But there's one EXTREMELY valuable reason I like most about walking.



Bilateral brain stimulation.



Like therapists use with EMDR-therapy, triggering the left and right side of the brain through peeps, ticks or eye-movement.



The same happens when you walk. Using the left and right side of your body while walking. With every step you take.

Connecting the more rational and more emotional part of your brain.



Thinking and feeling connects.



Which makes the process of clients go very deep, naturally.



Just like Andrea experienced.



Makes me think about a colleague of mine.

Klaske, a relationship therapist.



Several years ago, she had a burnout.



While she was in the middle of that painful exhausting experience, she decided to start walking in nature for 15 minutes.



More wasn't possible.



Then everyday she did a bit more.



15 minutes, became half an hour … one hour … two … three …



Up and up … until she walked six hours a day in nature. 



Within 2 months, she recovered from that burnout completely.

WITHOUT the support of a professional.



This brings me to that third reason that made Andrea's experience so deep.



Because as research shows, the effects of nature and walking work on the mental, emotional and physical level.



So, that's three important different levels.



Very effective in helping clients with their problems.



But …



… what do you think happens with yourself? The helping professional.



Well … 



Exactly the same.



Imagine what happens while working in nature …



Bi-lateral brain stimulation taking place.



Breathing in that fresh air.



Decreasing those stress levels.



Getting more connected with your body.



Intuition and creativity starting to open up.



You have the same benefits.

Not only personally. Also professionally.



This process can bring you and your profession to a whole new level.

Very naturally.



That doesn't mean that working at the office or practice doesn't go deep.

But it misses out on those benefits and different levels that are triggered naturally.



And nature and walking are just the tip of the iceberg.



So if you think about it:



Most of the helping professionals work inside.

Helping their clients to the best of their abilities.



Supporting them to get mentally, emotionally and physically healthy, and happy.



While research shows that "nature is not only nice to have, but it’s a MUST-HAVE for physical, emotional & mental health and cognitive function."



That feels a bit strange, right?!

Knowing what you know now.



As research shows, when helping professionals start to take their clients out into nature, this will have an enormous impact.

Not only on their clients' results. Also on themselves.



For now I kept things simple. 



First of all, you could see the enormous impact walking and talking in nature have.



But not only that …



… also, to give you this gift of easy yet powerful insight.

So you can start right away exploring yourself.

Taking a client, or a friend or colleague, outside and experiencing the difference.



But having said that, there's still more to it.



Lots more benefits.



Loads of opportunities and possibilities.



Endless potential and creativity.



And …



… there is a way to use nature itself in the process of helping others.

Which is very powerful and makes impact.



So let's keep on going on this adventurous journey.



I've created a three-part email series about those possibilities and how nature can be integrated in the process of helping others.



If you want to know more, click here to opt in.









FACEBOOK AD



What if coaches, counsellors and therapists could make much more impact on their clients than they do right now while being in the environment they love most?



As helping professionals, we always want to help others to the best of our abilities. That's at the core of our being.



Always wanting to learn and grow ourselves.



So we can be that snowflake. That creates an avalanche for our clients. 



Contributing to the people who need us most. Helping them grow and solve their problems. 



Always trying to give it our best. Fulfilling our purpose.



Just like Andrea.



An award-winning international life & business coach and speaker from Germany.



She helps others find clarity. Unlock their potential. Overcome barriers. Prevent burnout. Bring balance in work and health. Find happiness and meaning in their careers and everyday lives.



She feels alive and inspired when helping others. Like all the managers, executives, high potentials and highly intelligent people, she coached already from many different countries.



"But on the other hand ... it can sometimes be a bit exhausting and stressful", she says.



"Making lots of hours inside the office. Experiencing pressure of targets and limited time. Wanting to help others more and better. Then there's the paperwork. And also keeping the organisation happy."



"That's why I try to take a break in the afternoon. Go for a walk outside and refill my cup of energy. Clear my head. And take some fresh air. 



Like I advise all my clients when they experience stress or mental health issues.



It's so beautiful outside. I love it! And then … back to work."



(With her backpack filled with different certificates and techniques she learned over the years.)



But ever since she had that one-time experience four years ago, there's that little voice inside her head saying:



"There is more. I can feel it. Much more impact, somehow … ".



And not only that. 



There's also a strange thing going on at the office that is at odds with her more profound longing.



Because Andrea wants to impact her clients. She wants to help them grow and solve their problems. Mentally, emotionally and physically.



What does she advise her clients that experience stress or mental health issues?



Go out into nature. Take a walk. Take some fresh air. Relax.



The odd thing is … 



… when taking a walk in nature does so much good. Why does she sit inside her office all the time?



Think about it.



Most helping professionals work inside their office or practice.



Andrea thinks about this too, since she had that one-time experience. 



That time she was right in the middle of a new coaching program: 'Working with IMPACT techniques', going deep.



Her trainer discussed the possibility of encountering unconventional subjects, like childhood trauma or abuse. They can pop up while working with a client on a 'normal' topic or problem. And could blow you away. When not having any experience with that.



Like an iceberg in water. With the tip above the surface. And deeper problems lying underneath.



And then … the iceberg tumbles … causing a BIG wave …



So, this trainer suggested practising talking about unconventional subjects, preparing yourself.



Andrea got herself a buddy. Who suggested going out for a walk in nature.



They both needed some fresh air, so they did …



 … walking and talking about 'sex'.



And that's when something AMAZING happened.



Something Andrea had never experienced before. In all her years of experience.



The conversation and coaching process went so deep.



MIND-BLOWING.



Quite surprised their conversation felt so natural while walking outside.



She enjoyed being in nature.



Just like she did when she was a little girl. She remembered how magical it was as a child in nature with her family. 



Feeling alive. Inspired. Hearing the wind rushing through the trees. Seeing birds playing.



"This was something so much deeper and easier than I ever experienced before", she said.



"Despite working systemically, and with picture materials. Which incredibly helps to advance putting words on things that you feel you can't grasp."



"But that wasn't even close to what I experienced during that simple walk and talk in nature. 



"What happened exactly, I can hardly describe. But I felt immediately: this could make a huge impact. Using all my techniques and experience outside somehow."



She thought of it as a really excellent idea, to go one step further than usual. Feeling that longing deep inside to make much more impact.



On the other hand, she felt a bit anxious about what colleagues would say. About being different. And about possible organisation's restrictions and rules …



But the longing pulled hardest, so she started Google-ing anyway.



Unfortunately, she didn't find anything that could support her on this journey. And three years passed …



So, let's go back to that fantastic experience she had. Doing what she loves most. In the environment, she loves most.



What was going on in that unique experience Andrea had?  That felt so much deeper and easier than she experienced ever before.



Well …



Three things happened, actually:



1. NATURE

2. WALKING

3. EFFECT



These three things made that coaching conversation a mind-blowing experience.



Up next, I will explain more about the impact those three things have. In fact, they benefit in three different ways, on three different levels. 



I reckon that you didn't come this far if you don't like nature and don't like making an impact on your clients.



But nevertheless, if you don't, then please do not click this ad. Then this is not for you.



But if you do … 



Click the ad to read more.



































































Aspiring entrepreneurs often fail because they don’t start with the foundation.

 

They ignore important elements. They don't find the right idea that is in sync with their interests or unique strengths,  they don’t define a niche of people where they can solve their problem, they don’t know if their product or target market has a willing audience who wants what they have and will pay for it, And, importantly they don't have a success mindset.

 

Would-be entrepreneurs decide to start a business. They know they want to create something, or even a side-hustle that will give them the freedom to be rid of a job they hate or just extra cash to feel more secure. They rush to take some preliminary steps like finding a name, designing a logo, forming a corporation, putting up a page on their website, and that’s when they get stuck. What do they do next?

 

Or, maybe they do put something together, but it never becomes profitable.

 

Often the business becomes a drag, they don't enjoy what they do or it's a lot of work for little reward. This was not the big promise of an online business.

 

I've found that many would –be entrepreneurs don't have any idea of what business they want to run or how to do it. They randomly pick an idea, perhaps from a list of the easiest businesses to start, and hope a way to make money will emerge.. They don't know how to proceed. They aren't making any progress. So they look for help.

 

They turn to reading books and following some business websites, and listening to podcasts. But, these don’t give them the answers they need.

 

Eventually they find the dozens of gurus who sell a program that promises success. They sell the flash. The sales pitch is always the same, a technique showing a product launch that makes thousands of dollars the first time, or the secret to build a world class website, the one copywriting secret that gets 10 X the conversions, the critical elements every sales page needs, and how to grow your business without spending any money. Sounds so easy.

 

But something’s missing. Where is the core, the basis of the business?

 

What the aspiring entrepreneur is lacking is the proper groundwork. They need the important elements that are a result of thinking it through and planning. Not jumping into the middle and looking for quick results.

 

Starting a business is not hard, but it does take work, a strategy and putting the right structure in place. Without these elements, chances of success are low. It's like building a house. You start with plans, the foundation and the frame comes before the details that make it special.

 

Here are the must haves.

 

First, the business idea is important and it’s not just any idea.

The idea is the basis of the whole business, so getting it right is important. The idea has to be something you are deeply interested in and you have the skills and talent for. Or, you are willing to spend time learning.

 

This idea has to be enjoyable and gratifying. Something you will keep doing even when it gets hard. It has to fit in with the lifestyle you desire.

 

Few people think about this, but if you start a business that doesn't fit your personality or is objectionable in some way, then it's going to get old fast.

Why even start the business if you don't like what you do or how you do it?  For instance, let's say you like the idea of working from home, you enjoy its calm surroundings and easy pace, but the business you want to start requires travelling and visiting clients. This is probably the wrong idea or it is set up in the wrong way for you.

 

Making the right choice is important.

 

Second, a business exists to either solve a problem or fulfill a need.

These are the only things they do.  So the idea has to deliver value to a group of people. Many times a new business owner wants to include everyone as his market because he's afraid of losing customers.

 

 Your group of interested people doesn't need to be very large. An audience only has to be big enough to have sufficient clients who will want what you have and pay you for it. That is the key to having the income you're looking for.  

 

But you can't solve everyone's needs, nor do you want to dilute what you offer so it’s too general and doesn't fit anyone well. By picking a niche you can dominate  you will be the authority in the field.

 

The question is: What do you know or do that creates something distinct and eliminates the competition by putting you in a category of one?

 

 

Third, an aspiring entrepreneur should never proceed without validating their idea. Validating an idea will prove that there is a sufficient number of people who want what you have and are willing to pay for it.



It proves that there is a market to create a resilient business, and the business can prosper and provide a reliable income.  The process of validating an idea answers important questions about how compelling the offer is before spending a lot of time and money on developing it further.

 

 

Fourth, is mindset and beliefs.

No one talks about mindset as being critical to the entrepreneur. Everything is about the latest technique and hack. Again, that's what the online sages sell.

But it's words that have limitless potential and power. That includes the words and thoughts we tell ourselves. 

 

You become what you think about all day long." ― Ralph Waldo Emerson

 

Words carry vibrations and energy which we respond to. There is a scientific reason for this.

 

As the brilliant scientist Nikola Tesla said "If you want to find the secrets of the universe, think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration." 

Another world famous genius agrees: "Everything in life is vibration" - Albert Einstein. 

 

This is rarely factored in.  If you develop this mindset and apply strong emotions to your thoughts and words you can create, build and succeed in your business. However, negative emotions will pertain too, so be careful how you think.

 

Your thoughts are as critical as finding the right idea, product, and market. You can learn to develop a thinking process which works to help you turn your vision into reality.

 

 

 

Who Finds This Important?

 

There are only a small number of would-be entrepreneurs that will follow this path. Most don't want to do the foundational work or spend time working on the core ideas, or strategy, or finding the right niche, validating their market, or even believing that this can really happen for them.

 

Most don’t even know there is another way. Many are in a hurry to get their sales page up and sell something. Unfortunately, that’s not the path to long-term success or creating a resilient and powerful business. 

 

But this is not a game. If you're one of the ones who is serious about the future of your business, your freedom, your security, your wealth, and your escape from the matrix, the right beginnings provide much better success than an unplanned attempt. 

 

So if starting a business is not a whim and you understand the benefits of laying down all the critical pieces. There’s more help available. Sign up here for more information by email.
